title: Auto-Polling Status
description:
Build a job status monitor that automatically polls an API endpoint at a regular interval and displays updates.

### Requirements

- Poll the API every 3 seconds using `setInterval`
- Display the current job status (pending, processing, completed, failed)
- Show a progress bar that updates based on API response
- Stop polling automatically when the job reaches a terminal state (completed or failed)
- Show the last updated timestamp
- A "Start Polling" button begins polling; a "Stop" button pauses it
- Clean up the interval on unmount

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Start button | `start-btn` |
| Stop button | `stop-btn` |
| Status text | `status` |
| Progress bar | `progress-bar` |
| Progress value | `progress-value` |
| Last updated | `last-updated` |
| Poll count | `poll-count` |
constraints:
- Must use setInterval (not recursive setTimeout)
- Must stop polling on terminal states
- Interval must be cleaned up on unmount
- Must handle fetch errors without crashing
- Progress should be a percentage (0-100)
hints: Store the interval ID in a ref so you can clear it from multiple places (stop button, terminal state, unmount).
Use a ref for the poll count too — or state if you want it to trigger re-renders.
Check the status in the fetch response handler and clear the interval if it's 'completed' or 'failed'.
